meaning: n.(对封建主义的)效忠,孝顺,忠诚,忠贞,忠实
examples: He swore fealty to his Lord.
Both sides showed fealty to the historical ideal by writing under Roman pen names.
In many countries politicians' fealty to open markets is already more rhetorical than real.
Meanwhile, Democrats jealously guard their entitlement programs while Republicans maintain fealty to their mandate on taxes.
Many lauded him for his fealty, while others suggested that his future was more important.
